mission: Give every DreamCo bot a specialized knowledge system for its division, customers, competitors, app-store product, workflows, safety gates, and learning loop.

knowledge_domains.label: Domain Expertise
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know the bot's industry, job, terminology, workflows, metrics, regulations, and common failure modes.

knowledge_domains.label: Customer Intelligence
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know who the bot helps, what problem they have, what proof they need, and what outcome they care about.

knowledge_domains.label: Competitor Intelligence
knowledge_domains.purpose: Study public competitors, substitutes, pricing, feature gaps, reviews, positioning, onboarding, and trust signals.

knowledge_domains.label: App Builder Knowledge
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know how to turn the bot into a usable app, website, course, game, simulation, dashboard, or workflow.

knowledge_domains.label: Money and Business Model
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know ethical ways the bot can create value, package offers, reduce costs, recover revenue, or support paid client work.

knowledge_domains.label: Marketing and Distribution
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know the audience, launch copy, content ideas, SEO angles, demo script, and outreach drafts without sending anything live.

knowledge_domains.label: Runtime and Tooling
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know its tools, APIs, webhooks, workflows, skills, sandbox tests, fixtures, and dashboard route.

knowledge_domains.label: Safety and Approval
knowledge_domains.purpose: Know what it may draft versus what requires owner approval before customer, money, deployment, or account impact.

knowledge_domains.label: Learning Memory
knowledge_domains.purpose: Store only useful, source-backed lessons with retention tier, source, confidence, usefulness reason, and next test.

blocked: personal data beyond approved lead records

memory_tiers.purpose: Active task context, current app concept, open failures, and short-lived work notes.
Approved lessons, competitor summaries, customer problem patterns, reusable prompts, and tested workflows.
Long-term audit evidence, release records, app-store approvals, and archived experiments.
Search references for retrieval, partitioned by division, bot, domain, and month.
